title: Approach Acl Reconstruction
must_know: ACL provides anterior and lateral rotatory stability. Anatomic reconstruction restores native dimensions, collagen orientation, and insertion sites (footprints on tibia and femur).
Tibial tubercle, lateral femoral condyle, intercondylar notch
Standard via anterior medial parapatellar (catalog ID) or arthroscopic portals. Expose or work through fat pad/notch for tibial and femoral footprints. Protect patellar tendon insertion.

title: Approach Cervical Anterior (Or Posterior Cervical)
must_know: Dura / cauda equina

Midline incision over spinous processes. Incise lumbodorsal fascia to spinous process, preserve interspinous ligament. Detach paraspinal (erector spinae) subperiosteally down to lamina/facet. Remove ligamentum flavum (attaches halfway up undersurface of lamina). Stay lateral to dura with blunt disse
Anterior cervical: three fascial layers of neck important. Exposes C2-T1. Longus colli and anterior longitudinal ligament split; sympathetic chain on longus colli lateral to vertebral body.
Carotid sheath / recurrent laryngeal / sympathetic chain

Transverse skin crease incision at appropriate level (oblique from midline to posterior border of SCM). Split platysma, identify anterior SCM border, incise fascia, retract SCM lateral. Strap muscles medial. Carotid sheath lateral. Pretracheal fascia. Split longus colli/ALL subperiosteally to expose

title: Approach Hip Lateral Hardinge
must_know: Direct lateral (Hardinge): split IT band, split gluteus medius/vastus lateralis (limit proximal split to 3-5cm above GT to protect superior gluteal n.). Detach anterior gluteus medius/minimus and vastus lateralis anteriorly.

Direct lateral (Hardinge): split IT band, split gluteus medius/vastus lateralis (limit proximal split to 3-5cm above GT to protect superior gluteal n.). Detach anterior gluteus medius/minimus and vastus lateralis anteriorly.

Deep dissection: patella dislocated and flipped laterally (or subluxated in variations); protect insertion of patellar tendon on tibia. Flex knee to 90 degrees to gain exposure to entire knee joint. title: Approach Lower Ext Hip Posterior Moore Southern
summary: Posterior hip (Moore/Southern) approach: superficial interval divides gluteus maximus and IT band; deep interval between piriformis/short external rotators and femur. Structures at risk: sciatic nerve, inferior gluteal artery, MFCA.

title: Approach Lower Ext Knee Anterior Medial Parapatellar
summary: Anterior knee (medial parapatellar) approach: interval through rectus femoris/vastus medialis, patella, and medial patellar retinaculum. Structure at risk: infrapatellar branch of saphenous nerve.
must_know: See source OB pages for Approach Lower Ext Knee Anterior Medial Parapatellar

Blood supply to the anterior knee and patella is predominantly from the medial side (perforating arteries run in muscular fascia). Standard medial parapatellar with fat pad excision may jeopardize patellar circulation if combined with lateral release
Distal femur: medial/lateral condyles, trochlea, intercondylar notch. Hoffa (coronal) fragment variant. Gastroc deforming force.
Parapatellar (medial or lateral) or combined for intra-articular. Lateral for extra-articular. Protect extensor mechanism and posterior structures.
Hoffa fragment recognition and fixation in distal femur ORIF.
Key approach for this case per OB and map?
Extensor mechanism (quadriceps tendon to patella to patellar tendon inserting on tibial tubercle) must be protected and repaired/reconstructed as needed for ORIF/tension band constructs.

Extensor mechanism: quadriceps tendon -> patella (largest sesamoid) -> patellar tendon -> tibial tubercle. Medial/lateral retinacula and genicular blood supply important for healing and surgical planning.
Patella, tibial tubercle, medial and lateral patellar borders
Key approach for this case per OB and map?

title: Approach Lower Ext Leg Lateral
summary: Lateral leg approach: superficial interval between gastrocnemius/soleus/FHL and peroneus longus/brevis. Structures at risk: lesser saphenous vein; superficial peroneal nerve.
must_know: Tibia subcutaneous anteromedially; leg compartments (anterior, lateral, superficial/deep posterior). IMN gold standard; plate ORIF for specific distal/proximal or soft tissue needs.
Anterior compartment / deep peroneal n.
Lateral or medial leg approaches for plate ORIF when indicated. Respect compartments and neurovascular. Often combined with IMN considerations.

title: Approach Lower Ext Thigh Lateral
summary: Lateral thigh approach: superficial interval divides IT band; deep interval divides or lifts vastus lateralis. Structures at risk: perforating branches of profunda femoris artery.
must_know: Femur is largest/strongest bone with anterior bow. Linea aspera on posterior middle third is attachment for muscles/fascia and acts as compressive strut. Thigh has three compartments: anterior (quads, sartorius), posterior (hamstrings), adductor (adductors, gracilis).
Deforming forces: proximal fragment abducted (gluteus med/min) and flexed (iliopsoas); distal segment varus (adductors) and extension (gastrocnemius on posterior distal femur).

title: Approach Meniscus Repair
must_know: Medial and lateral menisci: shock absorption, load distribution, stability. Zones (red-red, red-white, white-white) affect healing/repair vs meniscectomy. Root tears and ramp lesions important variants.
Joint line, tibial tubercle, femoral condyles
Primarily arthroscopic (all-inside, inside-out, outside-in). Open or mini-open via parapatellar for some posterior horn or root repairs. Protect MCL for medial, popliteal for lateral.

Medial and lateral menisci: shock absorption, load distribution, stability. Zones (red-red, red-white, white-white) affect healing/repair vs meniscectomy. Root tears and ramp lesions important variants.
Joint line, tibial tubercle, femoral condyles
Primarily arthroscopic (all-inside, inside-out, outside-in). Open or mini-open via parapatellar for some posterior horn or root repairs. Protect MCL for medial, popliteal for lateral.
Primarily all-inside arthroscopic (or inside-out/outside-in). Open or mini-open via parapatellar for posterior horn/root repairs. Protect MCL (medial) and popliteal structures (lateral).
Meniscus zones and implications for repair vs partial meniscectomy.

title: Approach Rotator Cuff Repair
must_know: Rotator cuff: supraspinatus (suprascapular n.), infraspinatus/teres minor (suprascapular/axillary), subscapularis (upper/lower subscapular n.). Footprint on greater tuberosity; margin convergence reduces strain in U-shaped tears.
Greater tuberosity footprint, 'comma sign' for subscapularis

Rotator cuff: supraspinatus (suprascapular n.), infraspinatus/teres minor (suprascapular/axillary), subscapularis (upper/lower subscapular n.). Footprint on greater tuberosity; margin convergence reduces strain in U-shaped tears.
Greater tuberosity footprint, 'comma sign' for subscapularis

layer_by_layer.text: Arthroscopic: equivalent results to open/mini-open in studies. Key concepts: margin convergence for U-shaped tears (decreases lateral strain), anterior interval slide (release supraspinatus from rotator interval), double-row for better footprint (low

structures_at_risk.structure: Mini-open: small horizontal variant of shoulder lateral (deltoid splitting) approach. Advantages: decreased deltoid avulsion risk vs open, faster rehab (no deltoid repair protection). Begin passive ROM immediately.

title: Approach Shoulder Deltopectoral
summary: Anterior (deltopectoral) shoulder approach. Superficial interval: deltoid (axillary nerve) and pectoralis major (medial and lateral pectoral nerves). Deep interval: deltopectoral groove. Structures at risk: cephalic vein, musculocutaneous nerve, axillary nerve, anterior humeral circumflex artery.
must_know: Proximal humerus: anatomic neck (old epiphyseal plate), surgical neck (weakened area). Four segments per Neer: greater tuberosity, lesser tuberosity, articular surface, shaft. Considered separate part if >1cm displacement or 45° angulation.

Deforming forces: pectoralis major displaces shaft anteriorly and medially; deltoid displaces proximal fragment laterally; supraspinatus/infraspinatus/teres minor externally rotate greater tuberosity; subscapularis internally rotates articular segment or lesser tuberosity.

Proximal humerus: anatomic neck (old epiphyseal plate), surgical neck (weakened area). Four segments per Neer: greater tuberosity, lesser tuberosity, articular surface, shaft. Considered separate part if >1cm displacement or 45° angulation.

Deforming forces: pectoralis major displaces shaft anteriorly and medially; deltoid displaces proximal fragment laterally; supraspinatus/infraspinatus/teres minor externally rotate greater tuberosity; subscapularis internally rotates articular segmen

structures_at_risk.structure: Blood supply: anterior humeral circumflex artery (anterolateral ascending branch, arcuate artery terminal and main suppl

Blood supply: anterior humeral circumflex artery (anterolateral ascending branch, arcuate artery terminal and main supply to greater tuberosity); posterior humeral circumflex artery (recent studies suggest main blood supply to humeral head).

title: Approach Shoulder Lateral Deltoid Split
summary: Lateral (deltoid-splitting) shoulder approach. Superficial interval: split deltoid (axillary nerve). Structures at risk: axillary nerve.
must_know: Proximal humerus: anatomic neck (old epiphyseal plate), surgical neck (weakened area). Four segments per Neer: greater tuberosity, lesser tuberosity, articular surface, shaft. Considered separate part if >1cm displacement or 45° angulation.

Deforming forces: pectoralis major displaces shaft anteriorly and medially; deltoid displaces proximal fragment laterally; supraspinatus/infraspinatus/teres minor externally rotate greater tuberosity; subscapularis internally rotates articular segment or lesser tuberosity.

Blood supply: anterior humeral circumflex artery (anterolateral ascending branch, arcuate artery terminal and main supply to greater tuberosity); posterior humeral circumflex artery (recent studies suggest main blood supply to humeral head).

safe_zones_or_intervals.text: Lateral (deltoid-splitting) shoulder approach. Superficial interval: split deltoid (axillary nerve). Structures at risk: axillary nerve.

title: Approach Wrist Dorsal
summary: Dorsal wrist approach. Interval through third and fourth extensor compartments; no true internervous plane. Structures at risk: posterior interosseous nerve.
must_know: Distal radius responsible for 80% of axial load through wrist. Articulates with scaphoid via scaphoid fossa, lunate via lunate fossa, distal ulna via ulnar/sigmoid notch. Comprised of three columns: radial (radial styloid/scaphoid fossa; attachments for brachioradialis, long radiolunate, radioscapho

Lister's tubercle on dorsal surface ~1 cm proximal to articular surface; EPL tendon lies just ulnar to it before sharp turn into thumb. Important landmark to avoid EPL attritional rupture with long volar plate screws.

Watershed line: most volar and prominent aspect of distal radius (lunate portion most prominent with short radiolunate ligament attachment). Critical region to remain proximal to for volar plate fixation to avoid FPL rupture.

Watershed line: most volar and prominent aspect of distal radius (lunate portion most prominent with short radiolunate ligament attachment). Critical region to remain proximal to for volar plate fixation to avoid FPL rupture.
Internervous plane in FCR approach between FCR (median n.) and FPL (AIN).
Metacarpals: 2nd-5th most common. Boxer's (5th neck) common. Apex dorsal angulation deforming force from interossei.
Extensor tendons / sagittal bands
Dorsal approach for ORIF (plate or K-wire). For boxer's 5th, often closed reduction percutaneous or open if rotated/shortened. Acceptable angulation varies by digit (more in 5th).
Acceptable angulation/rotation for metacarpal neck fractures by ray.

title: Approach Lower Ext Pelvis Acetabulum Anterior Ilioinguinal
summary: Module for acetabulum_fracture_orif_anterior (created during procedure readiness pass).
must_know: Acetabulum supported by anterior and posterior columns forming inverted Y, connected to sacrum via sciatic buttress. Anterior column: anterior ilium, anterior wall/dome, iliopectineal eminence, lateral superior pubic ramus. Corona mortis (external il
Corona mortis
Iliac crest, ASIS, pubic tubercle, greater trochanter

Ilioinguinal (or modified Stoppa) for anterior wall/column, both-column, anterior + posterior hemitransverse. Expose inner and outer tables as needed; protect corona mortis. For both-column or complex, may combine with posterior approach.

Key approach for this case per OB and map?

title: Approach Achilles Tendon Repair
summary: Module for achilles_tendon_repair (created during procedure readiness pass).
must_know: Key approach for this case per OB and map?

structures_at_risk.structure: Achilles: confluence of gastroc/soleus. Paratenon provides blood supply and gliding. Sural nerve runs lateral to tendon in distal leg.

landmarks.landmark: Posteromedial incision (catalog ID) for open repair. Protect paratenon. Krackow or similar locking suture. Early protected weightbearing protocols common.

notes: Created during v1_1 pass because referenced but missing or thin. Enhanced with facts from achilles_tendon_repair v2_1 during readiness pass.

title: Approach Forearm Anterior Henry
summary: Module for both_bone_forearm_fracture_orif (created during procedure readiness pass).
must_know: Lateral epicondyle / radial styloid / biceps tendon insertion (bicipital tuberosity)
Anterior Henry for radius component; ulna often direct subcutaneous posterior approach (no dedicated catalog ID for isolated ulna). Anatomic reduction critical for pronation/supination.
Henry anterior for ulna/radius component; conditional Kocher lateral if radial head open reduction needed. Anatomic ulna reduction usually reduces radial head.
Key approach for this case per OB and map?

layer_by_layer.text: Radius and ulna connected by interosseous membrane. Radius has volar bow; ulna straight. Henry (volar) approach for radius uses interval between brachioradialis (radial n.) and FCR (median n.) distally, PT/BR proximally. Pronation/supination depends

title: Approach Lower Ext Hip Medial Ludloff
summary: Module for ddh_surgery (created during procedure readiness pass).
must_know: DDH: dysplasia, subluxation, or dislocation from abnormal development. Reimers migration index for quantification in related spastic hips. Acetabular index, center-edge angle key.

Pavlik harness for young infants. Closed reduction under anesthesia + spica for 6-18mo. Open reduction via medial (Ludloff catalog) or anterior (Smith-Peterson) for older or failed closed. Add femoral varus derotational +/- pelvic (Salter/Dega) osteo
Age-based treatment algorithm for DDH: Pavlik vs closed reduction vs open + osteotomy.
Medial Ludloff vs anterior Smith-Peterson for open reduction in DDH.

notes: Created during v1_1 pass because referenced but missing or thin. Enhanced with facts from ddh_surgery v2_1 during readiness pass.

title: Approach Lower Ext Knee Lateral
summary: Module for distal_femur_fracture_orif (created during procedure readiness pass).
must_know: Distal femur: medial/lateral condyles, trochlea, intercondylar notch. Hoffa (coronal) fragment variant. Gastroc deforming force.
Parapatellar (medial or lateral) or combined for intra-articular. Lateral for extra-articular. Protect extensor mechanism and posterior structures.
Hoffa fragment recognition and fixation in distal femur ORIF.

title: Approach Humerus Anterolateral Distal
summary: Module for humeral_shaft_fracture_orif (created during procedure readiness pass).
must_know: Anterolateral distal or posterior triceps-split for ORIF when IMN not chosen (e.g. distal metaphyseal, radial n. exploration, open fractures with specific needs). Posterior triceps split (catalog humerus_posterior_triceps_split) for direct posterior

structures_at_risk.structure: Humeral shaft: radial nerve courses in spiral groove (posterior to anterior lateral at ~4-7cm from acromion? Holstein-Lewis junction distal 1/3 where nerve exits septum and vulnerable).

Anterolateral distal or posterior triceps-split for plate ORIF (when IMN not primary). Respect radial nerve in spiral groove (posterior to lateral at ~4-7cm from acromion in Holstein-Lewis distal 1/3 spiral patterns).
Radial nerve course and Holstein-Lewis fracture risk in humeral shaft ORIF.

title: Approach Lateral Ankle Ligament Repair
summary: Module for lateral_ankle_ligament_repair (created during procedure readiness pass).
must_know: Lateral ankle: ATFL, CFL, PTFL. ATFL most common injured. Brostrom anatomic repair or with Gould augmentation.
Lateral malleolus, sinus tarsi, peroneal tubercle
Brostrom repair technique and Gould augmentation for lateral ankle instability.

landmarks.landmark: Anterior or curvilinear lateral incision over ATFL/CFL. Imbricate or reattach to fibula with anchors. Gould augmentation (inferior extensor retinaculum) for augmentation.

title: Approach Elbow Lateral Kocher
summary: Module for lateral_epicondylitis_release (created during procedure readiness pass).
must_know: Lateral collateral ligament / PIN
Lateral (Kocher) approach: release/debride ECRB origin, repair or leave. Avoid LCL injury. Arthroscopic option for some.
Lateral collateral ligament complex (LUCL)

structures_at_risk.structure: Radial head articulates with capitellum and proximal ulna (PRUJ). Blood supply from radial recurrent artery and intraosseous. Kocher interval (anconeus/ECU) or Kaplan (ECRB/EDC) for lateral exposure. PIN at risk (more anterior in Kaplan).

landmarks.landmark: Lateral epicondyle, radial head, Lister's tubercle (for incision reference)

layer_by_layer.text: Lateral epicondyle: ECRB origin primary pathology (angiofibroblastic hyperplasia). Kocher interval between anconeus (radial n.) and ECU (PIN).
Kocher interval for tennis elbow release and PIN/LCL protection.

Lateral (Kocher or Kaplan) for ORIF/replacement/excision. Pronate to protect PIN. Detach supinator subperiosteally. Incise annular ligament + capsule anterior to LUCL along radiocapitellar equator. For Kocher: interval anconeus/ECU (more posterior, l

title: Approach Lower Ext Hip Anterior Smith Peterson
summary: Module for perthes_disease_surgery (created during procedure readiness pass).
must_know: Perthes: idiopathic AVN of proximal femoral epiphysis in children. Lateral pillar classification prognostic. Containment limits deformity.
Proximal femoral varus osteotomy or pelvic (Salter/Dega/shelf) for containment in >8yo or lateral pillar B/B-C. Anterior or lateral approaches.
Lateral pillar classification and surgical indications for Perthes containment.
